instructions.instruction: Always use Declarative Pipeline syntax for new pipelines (`pipeline { agent any stages { ... } }`); never use Scripted Pipeline syntax (`node { ... }`) for new work.
instructions.original_snippets: NEVER use Scripted Pipeline for new work... BAD: New pipelines starting with `node { ... }`. GOOD: Start with `pipeline { agent any stages { ... } }` Declarative syntax.
instructions.relevant_when: When generating any new Jenkinsfile pipeline

instructions.instruction: Never store credentials as plain-text pipeline parameters; always use the Jenkins Credentials Store via `withCredentials` or the `credentials()` binding in the environment block.
instructions.original_snippets: NEVER store credentials as plain text pipeline parameters... BAD: `parameters { string(name: 'API_KEY', ...) }`... GOOD: `withCredentials([string(credentialsId: 'api-key-prod', variable: 'API_KEY')]) { ... }`
instructions.relevant_when: When the pipeline needs to use secrets, API keys, passwords, or tokens

instructions.instruction: Always add `parallelsAlwaysFailFast()` in the pipeline `options {}` block when using parallel or matrix blocks.
instructions.original_snippets: Always add `parallelsAlwaysFailFast()` in options when using parallel blocks... Always add `parallelsAlwaysFailFast()` to pipeline `options {}` block — covers all parallel/matrix blocks automatically.
instructions.relevant_when: When generating a Jenkinsfile that includes parallel stages or a matrix block

instructions.instruction: Always include `fingerprint: true` when using `archiveArtifacts` for build traceability.
instructions.original_snippets: Always include `fingerprint: true` when using `archiveArtifacts`... success { archiveArtifacts artifacts: '**/*.jar', fingerprint: true }
instructions.relevant_when: When generating a pipeline that archives build artifacts

instructions.instruction: Always include a `post { always { cleanWs() } }` block in every Declarative pipeline.
instructions.original_snippets: NEVER omit `post { always { cleanWs() } }`... BAD: No `post` block in the pipeline. GOOD: `post { always { cleanWs() } }` in every Declarative pipeline.
instructions.relevant_when: When generating any Declarative Jenkinsfile

instructions.instruction: Use single-quote shell strings with `withCredentials` to prevent Groovy string expansion of secrets: `sh 'curl -H "Authorization: Bearer $API_KEY"'` (single quotes prevent Groovy interpolation; the credential is still available via the shell environment).
instructions.original_snippets: NEVER call `sh` with inline secret variable expansion... BAD: `sh "curl -H 'Authorization: Bearer ${API_KEY}'"`... GOOD: `withCredentials([...]) { sh 'curl -H "Authorization: Bearer $API_KEY"' }` (single quotes prevent Groovy expansion)
instructions.relevant_when: When writing shell steps that reference credential variables

instructions.instruction: Extract independent stages (lint, unit-test, integration-test, SAST) into `parallel { }` blocks instead of sequential stages.
instructions.original_snippets: NEVER run all stages on a heavyweight executor without parallelism... BAD: Lint, unit-test, integration-test, and SAST in sequential stages. GOOD: Wrap independent stages in `parallel { stage('Lint') { ... } stage('Unit Test') { ... } }`.
instructions.relevant_when: When generating pipelines with multiple independent testing or checking stages

instructions.instruction: Include `timeout`, `buildDiscarder`, `timestamps`, and `disableConcurrentBuilds` in the pipeline `options {}` block.
instructions.original_snippets:
Options: timeout, buildDiscarder, timestamps, disableConcurrentBuilds
options {
    buildDiscarder(logRotator(numToKeepStr: '10'))
    timeout(time: 1, unit: 'HOURS')
    disableConcurrentBuilds()
    timestamps()
}
instructions.relevant_when: When generating any Declarative Jenkinsfile

instructions.instruction: Use `beforeAgent true` on `when` conditions to skip agent allocation if the condition fails.
instructions.original_snippets: Add `beforeAgent true` to skip agent allocation if condition fails.
instructions.relevant_when: When generating conditional stages that use a `when {}` block

instructions.instruction: Place `input` outside `steps {}` to avoid holding agents during manual approval.
instructions.original_snippets: Place `input` outside steps to avoid holding agents.
instructions.relevant_when: When generating a pipeline with a manual approval or input step

instructions.instruction: Use `catchError(buildResult: 'UNSTABLE', stageResult: 'FAILURE')` or `warnError` for error-tolerant stages rather than failing the entire build.
instructions.original_snippets:
catchError(buildResult: 'UNSTABLE', stageResult: 'FAILURE') { sh '...' }
warnError('msg') { sh '...' }
instructions.relevant_when: When generating pipelines with flaky or optional stages that should not fail the whole build

instructions.instruction: Use `@NonCPS` annotation for non-serializable operations (JsonSlurper, iterators, regex Matchers) in Scripted pipelines; do not include pipeline steps inside a `@NonCPS` method.
instructions.relevant_when: When generating Scripted pipeline methods that use non-serializable Groovy constructs

instructions.instruction: Use `python3 scripts/generate_declarative.py` for generating simple standard Declarative pipelines; use `python3 scripts/generate_scripted.py` for simple Scripted pipelines; use `python3 scripts/generate_shared_library.py` for shared library scaffolding.
instructions.original_snippets:
python3 scripts/generate_declarative.py --output Jenkinsfile --stages build,test,deploy --agent docker
python3 scripts/generate_scripted.py --output Jenkinsfile --stages build,test --agent label:linux
python3 scripts/generate_shared_library.py --name my-library --package com.example
instructions.relevant_when: When generating a simple standard pipeline or shared library scaffold

instructions.instruction: Always validate the generated Jenkinsfile using `devops-skills:jenkinsfile-validator` and then handle results: fix ERRORS, should fix WARNINGS, apply INFO optimisations based on use case; re-validate after fixes.
instructions.original_snippets: ALWAYS validate using devops-skills:jenkinsfile-validator skill... Handle validation results by severity: ERRORS: Must fix... WARNINGS: Should fix... INFO: Apply optimizations... Re-validate after fixes
instructions.relevant_when: After generating any Jenkinsfile

instructions.instruction: Consult `assets/templates/declarative/basic.Jenkinsfile` and `references/best_practices.md` before generating a Declarative pipeline.
instructions.original_snippets: Consult `assets/templates/declarative/basic.Jenkinsfile` and `references/best_practices.md`
instructions.relevant_when: When starting to generate a Declarative pipeline

instructions.instruction: Use `withCredentials` with credentials binding for secrets in both Declarative and Scripted pipelines.
instructions.original_snippets: withCredentials([string(credentialsId: 'key', variable: 'KEY')]) { sh 'curl -H "Auth: $KEY" ...' }
instructions.relevant_when: Whenever a shell step needs to use a secret value

instructions.instruction: Consult external plugin documentation for plugins not covered in `references/common_plugins.md` or for version-specific or complex configurations; skip lookup for basic well-documented steps.
instructions.original_snippets: Always consult external docs for: Plugins NOT in `references/common_plugins.md`... Skip external lookup when: Using basic syntax from `references/common_plugins.md`
instructions.relevant_when: When the pipeline uses a plugin that is not one of the standard covered plugins

instructions.instruction: Add SonarQube, OWASP Dependency-Check, or Trivy stages with fail thresholds for DevSecOps pipelines.
instructions.original_snippets: Add SonarQube, OWASP Dependency-Check, Trivy stages with fail thresholds.
instructions.relevant_when: When generating a pipeline that requires security scanning

instructions.instruction: Use `durabilityHint('PERFORMANCE_OPTIMIZED')` in options for simple pipelines to achieve 2-6x faster execution.
instructions.original_snippets: durabilityHint('PERFORMANCE_OPTIMIZED')  // 2-6x faster for simple pipelines
instructions.relevant_when: When generating simple pipelines where durability is not a primary concern

instructions.instruction: Use `matrix {}` with `axes {}` for multi-dimensional builds across platforms or browsers.
instructions.relevant_when: When generating a pipeline that needs to test across multiple OS, language version, or browser combinations
